have h_main : ∃ proj, as_projective q = ok proj ∧ (let X := Field51_as_Nat q.X; let Y := Field51_as_Nat q.Y; let Z := Field51_as_Nat q.Z; let T := Field51_as_Nat q.T; let X' := Field51_as_Nat proj.X; let Y' := Field51_as_Nat proj.Y; let Z' := Field51_as_Nat proj.Z; X' % p = (X * T) % p ∧ Y' % p = (Y * Z) % p ∧ Z' % p = (Z * T) % p) := by
    use q.as_projective
    <;> simp_all [as_projective]
    <;>
    (try decide) <;>
    (try ring_nf) <;>
    (try norm_num) <;>
    (try omega)
    <;>
    (try
      {
        have h₁ := h_qX_bounds 0 (by norm_num)
        have h₂ := h_qX_bounds 1 (by norm_num)
        have h₃ := h_qX_bounds 2 (by norm_num)
        have h₄ := h_qX_bounds 3 (by norm_num)
        have h₅ := h_qX_bounds 4 (by norm_num)
        have h₆ := h_qY_bounds 0 (by norm_num)
        have h₇ := h_qY_bounds 1 (by norm_num)
        have h₈ := h_qY_bounds 2 (by norm_num)
        have h₉ := h_qY_bounds 3 (by norm_num)
        have h₁₀ := h_qY_bounds 4 (by norm_num)
        have h₁₁ := h_qZ_bounds 0 (by norm_num)
        have h₁₂ := h_qZ_bounds 1 (by norm_num)
        have h₁₃ := h_qZ_bounds 2 (by norm_num)
        have h₁₄ := h_qZ_bounds 3 (by norm_num)
        have h₁₅ := h_qZ_bounds 4 (by norm_num)
        have h₁₆ := h_qT_bounds 0 (by norm_num)
        have h₁₇ := h_qT_bounds 1 (by norm_num)
        have h₁₈ := h_qT_bounds 2 (by norm_num)
        have h₁₉ := h_qT_bounds 3 (by norm_num)
        have h₂₀ := h_qT_bounds 4 (by norm_num)
        norm_num [Field51_as_Nat] at *
        <;>
        (try omega) <;>
        (try ring_nf at *) <;>
        (try norm_num at *) <;>
        (try omega)
      })
    <;>
    (try
      {
        simp_all [Field51_as_Nat]
        <;>
        norm_num
        <;>
        (try omega)
      })
    <;>
    (try
      {
        simp_all [Field51_as_Nat]
        <;>
        ring_nf
        <;>
        norm_num
        <;>
        (try omega)
      })
  
  obtain ⟨proj, h_proj, h_mod⟩ := h_main
  refine' ⟨proj, h_proj, _⟩
  simpa using h_mod

have h₀ : ∃ (proj : ProjectivePoint), q.as_projective = ok proj ∧ ((Field51_as_Nat proj.X) % p = (Field51_as_Nat q.X * Field51_as_Nat q.T) % p ∧ (Field51_as_Nat proj.Y) % p = (Field51_as_Nat q.Y * Field51_as_Nat q.Z) % p ∧ (Field51_as_Nat proj.Z) % p = (Field51_as_Nat q.Z * Field51_as_Nat q.T) % p) := by
    classical
    match q.as_projective with
    | ok proj =>
      refine' ⟨proj, rfl, _⟩
      -- Verify the formulas for X', Y', Z' in terms of X, Y, Z, T
      <;>
      simp_all [ProjectivePoint.ext_iff, CompletedPoint.as_projective, p]
      <;>
      norm_num [Field51_as_Nat_eq_foldr, Nat.mul_mod, Nat.add_mod, Nat.mod_mod]
      <;>
      ring_nf at *
      <;>
      norm_num at *
      <;>
      (try omega)
      <;>
      (try
        {
          -- Use the given bounds to simplify the problem
          have h₁ := h_qX_bounds 0 (by norm_num)
          have h₂ := h_qY_bounds 0 (by norm_num)
          have h₃ := h_qZ_bounds 0 (by norm_num)
          have h₄ := h_qT_bounds 0 (by norm_num)
          simp_all [Field51_as_Nat_eq_foldr]
          <;>
          norm_num at *
          <;>
          omega
        })
      <;>
      (try
        {
          -- Use the given bounds to simplify the problem
          have h₁ := h_qX_bounds 1 (by norm_num)
          have h₂ := h_qY_bounds 1 (by norm_num)
          have h₃ := h_qZ_bounds 1 (by norm_num)
          have h₄ := h_qT_bounds 1 (by norm_num)
          simp_all [Field51_as_Nat_eq_foldr]
          <;>
          norm_num at *
          <;>
          omega
        })
      <;>
      (try
        {
          -- Use the given bounds to simplify the problem
          have h₁ := h_qX_bounds 2 (by norm_num)
          have h₂ := h_qY_bounds 2 (by norm_num)
          have h₃ := h_qZ_bounds 2 (by norm_num)
          have h₄ := h_qT_bounds 2 (by norm_num)
          simp_all [Field51_as_Nat_eq_foldr]
          <;>
          norm_num at *
          <;>
          omega
        })
      <;>
      (try
        {
          -- Use the given bounds to simplify the problem
          have h₁ := h_qX_bounds 3 (by norm_num)
          have h₂ := h_qY_bounds 3 (by norm_num)
          have h₃ := h_qZ_bounds 3 (by norm_num)
          have h₄ := h_qT_bounds 3 (by norm_num)
          simp_all [Field51_as_Nat_eq_foldr]
          <;>
          norm_num at *
          <;>
          omega
        })
      <;>
      (try
        {
          -- Use the given bounds to simplify the problem
          have h₁ := h_qX_bounds 4 (by norm_num)
          have h₂ := h_qY_bounds 4 (by norm_num)
          have h₃ := h_qZ_bounds 4 (by norm_num)
          have h₄ := h_qT_bounds 4 (by norm_num)
          simp_all [Field51_as_Nat_eq_foldr]
          <;>
          norm_num at *
          <;>
          omega
        })
    | err h =>
      exfalso
      -- Since `q` is a `CompletedPoint`, `q.as_projective` should not be an error
      -- This case is impossible because `as_projective` always returns a valid `ProjectivePoint`
      simp_all [CompletedPoint.as_projective]
  -- Use the result from h₀ to complete the proof
  obtain ⟨proj, h₁, h₂⟩ := h₀
  refine' ⟨proj, _⟩
  constructor
  · -- Prove that as_projective q = ok proj
    exact h₁
  · -- Prove the congruences for X', Y', Z'

    (try omega)

    <;>

    <;>
    (try omega)

have h₁ : ∃ proj, as_projective q = ok proj := by
    -- The `as_projective` function always returns a valid `ProjectivePoint`.
    cases q.as_projective
    · -- If it returns a `Result.ok`, we directly get the `ProjectivePoint`.
      exact ⟨( q.as_projective ), by simp⟩
    · -- If it returns a `Result.noTea`, we derive a contradiction because `as_projective` should always succeed.
      exfalso
      -- This case should not be reachable because `as_projective` is guaranteed to return a valid `ProjectivePoint`.
      -- The actual implementation of `as_projective` would not produce a `Result.noTea` error.
      -- Therefore, this branch is impossible.
      cases q <;> simp_all (config := {decide := true})
  -- We obtain the `ProjectivePoint` from the previous step.
  obtain ⟨proj, h₂⟩ := h₁
  -- We refine the goal to prove the main statement with the obtained `proj`.
  refine' ⟨proj, h₂, ?_⟩
  -- We use trivial arithmetic properties to satisfy the remaining conditions.
  <;>
  (try decide) <;>
  (try simp_all [Nat.mod_eq_of_lt]) <;>
  (try ring_nf) <;>
